sourceMovie.title: Wild at Heart
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Wild at Heart for the same reason it broke through in 1990: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. David Lynch keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Nightmare Alley
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wild at Heart clicked for you, Nightmare Alley (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: One Battle After Another
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wild at Heart clicked for you, One Battle After Another (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wild at Heart clicked for you, Safe Haven (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and romance, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wild at Heart?
faq.answer: Try Nightmare Alley, One Battle After Another, Safe Haven—each shares DNA with Wild at Heart while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wild at Heart?
faq.answer: Wild at Heart (1990) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wild at Heart worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
